metadata.objective: Build a native macOS text editor that feels as fast as Unix tools such as less, more, tail, grep, awk, sort, uniq and sed while also supporting a practical edit mode.

modes.name: ViewAnalysisMode
modes.description: Read-only, maximum-speed mode for opening, viewing, searching, filtering, following, inspecting and analyzing very large files.
modes.save: not allowed except export/copy/extract pipeline results
modes.performance_model: mmap or streaming + lazy line index + visible viewport only

modes.name: EditMode
modes.description: Editable mode for normal and medium files with safe writes, undo/redo, syntax highlighting, search/replace, multi-cursor and block selection.
modes.performance_model: rope or piece table buffer + incremental rendering + background parsing

core_product_decision.explicit_rule: There are only two user-facing modes: View/Analysis Mode and Edit Mode.

unix_principles_to_adapt.principle: Do not read the entire file unless needed
unix_principles_to_adapt.application_rule: Opening a huge file should only read metadata and the bytes required for the first viewport.

unix_principles_to_adapt.principle: Stream sequentially for search and analysis
unix_principles_to_adapt.application_rule: Search, filters, extraction, stats and pipelines operate chunk-by-chunk and emit results progressively.

unix_principles_to_adapt.principle: Use byte offsets as stable positions
unix_principles_to_adapt.application_rule: Large-file references should use byte offsets first, with line numbers built lazily.
Analysis pipelines should compose filter, extract, transform, group, count and export stages.
Results appear as soon as they are found; the user must not wait for complete scans.
Syntax highlighting, full statistics, indexing and minimap are optional background tasks, never prerequisites for viewing.
Every long-running operation has a cancellation token and visible cancel button.

global_performance_budgets.perceived_open_multiple_10gb_files_targe: Each file opens by metadata + mmap/stream handle + first viewport only; never full-read.
global_performance_budgets.ram_for_single_10gb_view_analysis_file_m: Target < 50-100 MB excluding OS page cache.
global_performance_budgets.ram_scaling_rule: RAM scales with visible regions, caches and indexes, not file size.
global_performance_budgets.background_task_rule: All background tasks must be cancellable and lower priority than UI.

modules.name: Mode Manager
modules.purpose: Owns the two user-facing modes and enforces capability differences between View/Analysis Mode and Edit Mode.
responsibilities: Block invalid commands in current mode
Convert View/Analysis Mode to Edit Mode when explicitly requested
Prevent accidental destructive operations on huge files

core_data_structures: CapabilitySet { can_edit, can_save, can_replace, can_search, can_filter, can_follow, can_export }
algorithms: Determine mode from size threshold, file type, user option and safety policy
Gate every command through capability check
Conversion requires building editable buffer and may be cancelled
edge_cases: Plugin tries edit in View/Analysis Mode

core_data_structures: FilePreflight { path, size_bytes, permissions, modified_at, inode, encoding_hint, binary_confidence }
algorithms: Open path -> metadata -> choose mmap or stream -> first viewport scan
Tail path -> seek near EOF -> scan backward for line boundaries
Read path -> check chunk cache -> mmap slice or disk read
Save path -> encode -> temp file -> fsync policy -> atomic rename

traceability_index.title: Only two user-facing modes exist: View/Analysis Mode and Edit Mode
Open huge files directly in View/Analysis Mode by default
Disable editing commands in View/Analysis Mode
Allow copy, search, filter, follow, inspect, bookmark, extract and export in View/Analysis Mode
Require explicit confirmation before converting huge file to Edit Mode
Show current mode in status bar and title metadata
Expose mode capabilities to command registry
Do not allow plugin commands to bypass mode restrictions
Opening a file must never require reading the whole file
Use memory mapping for View/Analysis Mode when safe
Use chunked streaming when mmap is unavailable or unsafe
Read only enough bytes to render initial viewport
Expose byte-offset based access API
Support tail-like reads from end of file
Detect file growth for follow mode
Detect truncation and log rotation
Perform atomic writes in Edit Mode
Never allocate buffer equal to file size in View/Analysis Mode
Do not build full line index during open
Build line index lazily for visible and searched regions
Support byte offset navigation without full line index
Support approximate percentage navigation
Support progressive jump-to-line with progress feedback
Cache line boundaries for visited chunks
Invalidate line index safely when file changes
Support mixed line endings with warnings
Initial viewport renders without full-file scan
Render only visible lines plus overscan
Support page down and page up
Support jump to start and end of file
Support jump to percentage of file
Support jump to byte offset
Support jump to line with progressive indexing
Scrolling must remain smooth while background tasks run
Extremely long lines must be horizontally virtualized
Search starts immediately without full indexing
Search emits results progressively
First result target under 200ms when match is near current/early region
Support literal search using fast algorithms
Support regex search using streaming-safe engine
Support chunk-boundary matches
Support before/after context lines like grep -C
Support invert match like grep -v
Support count-only mode like grep -c
Support match-only extraction like grep -o
Support cancellation like Ctrl+C
Support bounded result buffer and export all results
Open file at end like tail
Follow appended lines like tail -f
Pause follow without closing file
Resume follow
Detect truncation
Detect log rotation
Apply live filters to followed data
Preserve manual scroll position when user scrolls away from bottom
Filter lines without modifying original file
Support contains, regex, AND, OR, NOT
Support grep -v style inverted filter
Support awk-like field extraction for delimited text
Support sort-like streamed or external sort strategy
Support uniq-like duplicate collapse
Support wc-like counts
Support reusable saved pipelines
Support progressive preview after each stage
Support export pipeline result as text, CSV or JSON
Pipeline execution must be cancellable
Pipeline must respect memory budget
Use rope or piece-table buffer in Edit Mode
Support insert, delete, replace
Support undo and redo as transactions
Support search and replace
Support regex replace with captures
Support block/column selection
Support multi-cursor editing
Support trim, sort, unique and line operations
Perform atomic save
Warn when file is too large for safe Edit Mode
Support replace in Edit Mode
Support regex capture replacement
Support replace preview before applying
Support replace all as one undo transaction
Support non-destructive transform preview in View/Analysis Mode
Support export transformed output without modifying original
Support line-based transformations: trim, case, prefix, suffix
Support safe cancellation for large transforms
Render visible lines only
Use overscan buffer
Cache line layouts
Render search highlights from byte ranges
Render bookmarks and markers
Render selections and block selections
Virtualize extremely long lines horizontally
Avoid full-document text measurement
Every long-running task must have cancellation token
UI tasks always outrank background analysis tasks
Search and follow tasks can run concurrently
Indexing pauses under memory pressure
Expose background task panel
Allow cancel from UI
Throttle progress updates
Recover from worker failure
Measure perceived open time
Measure first viewport render time
Measure scroll frame time
Measure search throughput MB/s
Measure memory per open file
Measure chunk cache hit rate
Provide benchmark suite for 1GB, 10GB and 20x10GB scenarios
Fail CI when performance budget regresses beyond threshold

summary.primary_goal: Perceived open time under 200ms for 10GB files via mmap/streaming and first-viewport-only rendering.

workspace_layout.crates/fastpad_app_macos: Native macOS shell and menu/window integration
workspace_layout.crates/fastpad_core: Document manager, mode manager, command registry
workspace_layout.crates/fastpad_file: mmap, streaming, atomic save, byte-offset reads
workspace_layout.crates/fastpad_line_index: lazy line indexing and offset mapping
workspace_layout.crates/fastpad_viewport: less/more style paging and viewport model

absolute_rules_for_codex: Do not load a 10GB file into a String.
Do not build a full line index before first render.
Do not syntax-highlight the full file before showing it.
Do not block the UI thread for file scanning, search, indexing or parsing.
Do not expose edit commands in View/Analysis Mode.
Do not let plugins bypass mode capabilities.
Use byte offsets for large-file references.
Every long-running task must support cancellation.
Benchmark large-file scenarios before declaring success.
